organization.organization_background: Utkrusht is a proof-of-skills marketplace that helps agile teams find talent with strong fundamental concepts with within days. We help aspiring professionals improve their profile with continuous improvement in their skills, documenting their journey and helping them fulfill their potential. We primarily build, conduct, proctor and disseminate proof-of-skill assessments in Technical domains like AI, ML, Fullstack development, High scale distributed systems, DevOps etc but do help non-tech roles like Sales, HR, Marketing etc

role_context: The role involves building and maintaining structured, accessible web pages using semantic HTML, forms, and responsive CSS layouts with Flexbox/Grid, while applying naming conventions, preprocessors, and frameworks to deliver scalable components. They will implement basic JavaScript for DOM manipulation, simple event handling, and introductory asynchronous patterns to support dynamic UI interactions, with an emphasis on debugging and performance optimization across browsers. The role expects independent completion of small to moderate tasks, refactoring existing code, and collaborating with teammates to ensure maintainability and SEO-friendly designs, while adhering to coding standards and version control practices. They should demonstrate growing proficiency in using dev tools, CSS specificity reasoning, and reusable component thinking to contribute to a cohesive front-end codebase.
questions_prompt:
## EMBEDDED TASK SPECIFICATION (USE VERBATIM) This competency uses a **fixed task specification**. The scenario, behavioural requirements, evaluation criteria, design reference, and starter code described below MUST be used exactly as written. Do NOT invent an alternative scenario, alternative requirements, alternative behaviour, or alternative starter code. The generated task is a faithful packaging of this specification into a deployable repository — not a re-imagining of it. --- ### Business Context **Nerdium** is hiring frontend developers. As part of the frontend screening they ask candidates to replicate a real-world popup component for the **Swim For Sick Kids** charity — a registration-prompt overlay that appears shortly after a visitor lands on the charity's homepage, encouraging them to sign up and claim a free swimming cap. The candidate's job is to complete the popup so that it matches the design reference, appears five seconds after page load, can be dismissed via its close icon, and routes the call-to-action button to the right destination in a new tab. --- ### Design Reference (the candidate MUST match this design) ![Free Swimming Cap popup design reference](https://drive.google.com/thumbnail?id=1g-SGZ4AqnBF-2vjGlytozk5Dlt4FGJYy&sz=w1200) Full-resolution view: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1g-SGZ4AqnBF-2vjGlytozk5Dlt4FGJYy/view **Visual elements visible in the reference (the candidate must replicate the look and feel):** - Dark navy / blue rounded-rectangle popup with subtle shadow - A small circular close icon (×) at the **top-right** corner of the popup - Yellow headline text: **FREE SWIMMING CAP!** - Short white subhead beneath the headline (single paragraph): *"Claim your free Swim For Sick Kids cap and welcome pack when you register today."* - Yellow tagline beneath the subhead: *"But hurry, stock is limited and going fast!"* - A circular product image in the centre showing a green swim cap with the text **"SWIM FOR SICK KIDS"** - A wide yellow rounded-rectangle call-to-action button at the bottom labelled **SIGN UP NOW!** in dark navy uppercase text - The popup is horizontally and vertically centred over the host page, with a semi-transparent dark backdrop dimming the rest of the page when the popup is visible --- ### Functional Requirements (REPLICATE EXACTLY) 1. **Host page** — a single, simple HTML page on which the popup will appear. The popup MUST be hidden on initial page load. 2. **Auto-show timing** — the popup MUST become visible **exactly 5 seconds after the page finishes loading**. Before 5 seconds it is hidden (not just transparent). The 5-second timer starts on `DOMContentLoaded` (or jQuery's `$(document).ready`) — either is acceptable. 3. **Close behaviour** — clicking the close icon (×) at the top-right of the popup MUST hide the popup AND the backdrop. After closing, the popup must not re-appear during that page session. The starter HTML wires the close icon to a global `closePopup()` function via `onclick="closePopup()"` — the candidate must DEFINE that function so the click actually dismisses the popup. 4. **Sign Up Now behaviour** — clicking the **SIGN UP NOW!** button MUST open `https://designerds.com.au` in a **new tab**. The starter HTML uses `href="/register/swim-for-sick-kids"` which is INTENTIONALLY WRONG — the candidate must either change the href to `https://designerds.com.au` (with `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"`) or use JS to intercept the click and call `window.open('https://designerds.com.au', '_blank')`. 5. **No frameworks** — pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript only. jQuery is permitted (the starter `index.html` loads it via CDN). React, Vue, Angular, Bootstrap, Tailwind, or any build tooling (npm, webpack, vite) are NOT permitted. --- ### STARTER CODE — USE VERBATIM AS THE CONTENTS OF THE REPOSITORY FILES The three blocks below are the EXACT starting content for the three repository files. The generated task's `code_files` MUST use these blocks **byte-for-byte**, with no edits, no added comments, no reformatting, no whitespace cleanup. Do NOT add `// TODO`, `// Implement` or any placeholder comments anywhere. The starter is intentionally minimal and intentionally incomplete — the missing styling, the empty JS, the wrong-link ``, and the unresolved `onclick="closePopup()"` are all part of the test. #### File: `index.html` — use this as the ENTIRE contents of index.html ```html Nerdium HTML + CSS + Javascript/Jquery Test

FREE SWIMMING CAP!

Claim your free Swim for Sick Kids cap and welcome pack when you register today.

But hurry, stock is limited and going fast!

SIGN UP NOW!
``` #### File: `styles.css` — use this as the ENTIRE contents of styles.css ```css body { font-family:Roboto; text-align:center; } .yellow{ color:#fbc72c; } .green { color:#85bd41; } ``` #### File: `script.js` — leave this file EMPTY (zero bytes) The candidate writes all JavaScript from scratch in `script.js`. Do NOT add comments, do NOT add `// TODO`, do NOT add a stub `closePopup` function. The file must be empty. --- ### Evaluation Criteria (what Nerdium is grading) The grading rubric is split into two equally weighted areas: 1. **HTML + CSS — attention to detail / fidelity to the design** - How close the candidate's popup looks to the design reference (colour palette, rounded corners, button styling, close-icon styling, font weights, vertical/horizontal centring, spacing, backdrop dimming). - The candidate is free to keep or restructure the starter HTML markup, but the visible result must match the design reference. - No `!important` abuse, no inline-style soup. 2. **JavaScript / jQuery fluency — popup logic** - Correct **5-second delayed appearance** on page load (the popup starts with the `.hidden` class; the candidate removes it after the timeout). - Correct **close behaviour**: defines a working `closePopup()` function (or replaces the inline `onclick` entirely with a cleaner event listener) so the × icon dismisses the popup and the backdrop. - Correct **Sign Up Now redirect**: clicking the CTA opens `https://designerds.com.au` in a **new tab**, NOT the starter's wrong `/register/swim-for-sick-kids` href. - Clean event wiring (`addEventListener` or jQuery `.on(...)`). --- ### Required Repository Structure This is a **browser-based** task — pure HTML/CSS/JS, no Docker, no build tooling, no server. The generated repository MUST contain exactly: - `index.html` — contents as in the STARTER CODE block above (byte-for-byte). - `styles.css` — contents as in the STARTER CODE block above (byte-for-byte). - `script.js` — EMPTY file (zero bytes). - `README.md` — see README requirements below. - `.gitignore` — standard web exclusions (IDE files, OS files, .DS_Store, etc.). Do NOT include `docker-compose.yml`, `run.sh`, `kill.sh`, `package.json`, `app.js`, or any backend / server / build files. Do NOT include any link to the original CodePen source anywhere in the generated repository — the candidate must not be pointed at the pen. --- ### README Requirements The generated README MUST contain, in this order: 1. **Task Overview** — 3-4 sentences describing the Nerdium / Swim For Sick Kids popup scenario per the Business Context above. 2. **Design Reference** — MUST embed the design image inline using EXACTLY this markdown: `![Design Reference](https://drive.google.com/thumbnail?id=1g-SGZ4AqnBF-2vjGlytozk5Dlt4FGJYy&sz=w1200)` followed by a fallback line: `Full-resolution: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1g-SGZ4AqnBF-2vjGlytozk5Dlt4FGJYy/view` 3. **Objectives** — 3-5 bullets covering: the 5-second delayed appearance, the close-icon dismissal (note that the starter wires `onclick="closePopup()"` which the candidate must DEFINE), the Sign Up Now redirect to `https://designerds.com.au` in a new tab (note that the starter href is intentionally wrong), and visual fidelity to the design reference. 4. **Helpful Tips** — 3-4 bullets nudging toward CSS positioning techniques for the modal, `setTimeout` for the 5-second delay, and event-driven JS for the close/CTA — without revealing the solution. Do NOT mention specific function names or APIs. 5. **How to Verify** — 3-5 bullets: open `index.html` in a browser, wait 5 s, popup appears; click ×, popup disappears; click Sign Up Now, designerds.com.au opens in a new tab; visual comparison against the embedded design reference. Do NOT include a "Boilerplate" section. Do NOT reference any external pen, sandbox, or starter URL. The starter code lives in the repository itself. --- The goal is to evaluate the candidate's ability to replicate a real-world popup design using BASIC HTML/CSS and JavaScript, while implementing simple timed-show + dismissal + redirect logic. Every requirement above is mandatory; no opt-out wording ("answer any N of M", "choose one of", etc.) belongs anywhere in the generated task.
